channel.title: subject on Mahmood's blog
channel.description: Recent content in subject on Mahmood's blog

item.title: discrete math
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rank}{rank}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) 1 set 1.1 disjoint sets two sets are said to be disjoint sets if they have no element in common 1.2 multiset a multiset is a modification of the concept of a set that, unlike a set, allows for multiple instances for each of its elements. the number of instances given for each element is called the multiplicity of that element in the multiset 2 ordered pair given \(a, b\) are mathematical objects, \((a,b)\) is an ordered pair, \((a,b) \neq (b,a)\), as an example, the \((x,y)\) coordinates on a function is an ordered pair 3 cartesian product cartesian product of 2 sets is the set of all the possible ordered pairs that can be obtained by taking an element from \(A\) as the first in the pair and an element from \(B\) as the second \begin{gather*} A \times B = \{(a,b) \ | \ a \in A \land b \in B\}\\ (a,b) \in A \times B \Leftrightarrow a \in A \land b \in B \end{gather*}
